D2_so2_unification.reading: F73 (rest,kinetic) and generation (common,differential) are the SAME SO(2) rotation; 45deg is the equal-split point of both -- one thread

D3_leptons_at_the_45deg_saturation.reading: the charged-lepton amplitude is AT the EM-rotation saturation = the F73 bound-pair stability cap

D4_em_selection_rule.verdict: only the clean EM-coupled charged leptons sit at 2/3; quarks are QCD-contaminated (off, and unequal); neutrinos (neutral, Majorana/seesaw F47) are not EM-pinned and their Q is ordering/scale-dependent. Charge is the selector.

D5_honest_residual.statement: EM explains the SELECTION (which sector sits at the critical point: only charged leptons couple to the clean abelian rotation) but NOT the magnitude: perturbative EM is ~340x too weak to rotate democratic(1/3)->equipartition(2/3). The value 45deg is a critical/saturation condition pinned by data; deriving it from the non-perturbative EM-driven gap (and the Cooper-pair singlet's own 1/sqrt2 structure) is the residual open question.
